expected_benefit: Lower repeated input tokens, better DeepSeek prefix cache reuse, lower warm-turn latency and cost.
performance_risk: Extra hashing/bookkeeping; stale prefix invalidation checks can add small CPU overhead.
quality_risk: Over-optimizing cache can omit fresh source truth.
hard_controls: Stable-prefix hash, drift reason, source-truth freshness guard, dynamic-tail boundary.
required_measurement: cacheHitRatePct trend, input token delta, stable prefix drift count, source-truth miss count.
execution_decision: P0 absorb into existing owner; do not create CacheFirstLoop runtime.

expected_benefit: Lower prompt bloat, lower TUI render cost, lower memory pressure, fewer cache-prefix breaks.
performance_risk: Artifact IO and preview generation overhead.
quality_risk: Preview too small can hide needed facts.
hard_controls: Artifact path, bounded preview, range reread fallback, evidence link in final report.
required_measurement: toolResultChars, artifact bytes, preview chars, reread count, failed verification caused by missing context.
execution_decision: P0 absorb and test across Read/Bash/PowerShell/MCP/Agent.

expected_benefit: Fewer failed tool turns, fewer retry loops, better DeepSeek tool-call reliability.
performance_risk: Repair parsing adds CPU and can add one retry turn when capped incorrectly.
quality_risk: Bad repair can execute wrong tool/args.
hard_controls: Max repair pass count, schema validation, identical-call storm breaker, visible repair evidence.
required_measurement: repair pass count, repaired/failed ratio, duplicate tool storm count, latency per repaired turn.
execution_decision: P0 absorb into adapter; no second tool runtime.

expected_benefit: Pro used only when Flash failure signals justify it; quality up without default cost explosion.
performance_risk: Signal collection overhead is tiny; over-triggering raises cost and latency.
quality_risk: False positives can waste Pro calls; false negatives can leave quality low.
hard_controls: Admission ledger, route reason, deny default Pro, post-run cost-quality board.
required_measurement: Pro admission count, cost delta, pass-after-escalation rate, false escalation review.

dsxu_owner: Query loop / tool dispatch / Tool metadata
expected_benefit: Lower wall-clock for independent read-only tools, better long-task responsiveness.
performance_risk: Concurrent FS/search can saturate IO; ordering bugs can hurt cache/log determinism.
quality_risk: Parallel writes or unsafe tools could corrupt state.
hard_controls: Only consecutive read-only/concurrencySafe calls, serial barrier for writes, deterministic result order.
required_measurement: wall-clock delta, IO wait, result order check, write-tool parallelization violations.
execution_decision: P0/P1 absorb carefully; never parallelize side-effect tools.

dsxu_owner: work-state timeline / TUI / CLI / final report
expected_benefit: User sees route/cost/cache/tool/permission/failure status; better trust and debuggability.
performance_risk: Too many events can slow TUI, worsen resize/scroll, or spam logs.
quality_risk: UI state can drift from actual execution if not same-source.
hard_controls: Single event source, throttled rendering, bounded operator summary, resize/scroll harness.
required_measurement: render latency, event count, resize regression, state drift checks, user-visible artifact links.
execution_decision: P0 absorb as projection only; no new UI stream.

expected_benefit: Better long-task recovery and source continuity; less transcript re-ingestion.
performance_risk: Disk/log growth, compact CPU, memory retention.
quality_risk: Old summary can override fresh source truth.
hard_controls: Append-only summaries, freshness expiry, source hash check, retention policy.
required_measurement: session log size, compact duration, resume success, stale evidence blocks.

expected_benefit: Parent gets concise summary/path/hash/evidence instead of transcript bloat.
quality_risk: Too little detail can hide worker mistakes.
hard_controls: Required worker scope, test evidence, artifact path, parent verifier rejection on partial evidence.
required_measurement: parent token delta, worker evidence completeness, unresolved risk count.
execution_decision: P1 absorb; no new agent runtime.

expected_benefit: Safer ecosystem intake and fewer surprise permissions/costs.
quality_risk: Overly broad negotiation can block useful tools.
hard_controls: Capability cache, permission boundary, version/fallback evidence, no standalone runtime.
required_measurement: MCP startup time, failed preflight count, permission denials, registry conflict count.
execution_decision: P1 absorb as registry evidence only.

expected_benefit: Reasonix benchmark files teach same-task raw transcript, cost, diff, and scoring package discipline.
performance_risk: Benchmark harness can be slow and IO-heavy.
quality_risk: Using their result as DSXU claim would be false.
hard_controls: Use method pattern only; DSXU must rerun own same-task raw evidence.
required_measurement: raw transcript count, scoring reproducibility, cost file, failure recovery chain.
execution_decision: P1 use as method; not product result claim.

expected_benefit: Native/Rust/heap-limit ideas can inspire startup profiling and memory guard.
performance_risk: Native dependency adds packaging complexity and cross-platform risk.
quality_risk: New native layer can break open-source installability.
hard_controls: Prefer JS/Bun profiling first; native only after measured bottleneck.
required_measurement: startup time, heap RSS, cold install smoke, Windows/macOS/Linux parity.
execution_decision: P2 review only; do not add native layer now.

expected_benefit: Better performance diagnosis and GitHub evidence story.
performance_risk: High-cardinality logging can slow TUI and grow files.
quality_risk: Telemetry can leak secrets or become marketing-only.
hard_controls: Redaction, sampling, opt-in/export boundary, artifact retention.
required_measurement: event write rate, log size, secret scan, telemetry off-mode smoke.
execution_decision: P1 absorb only through evidence owner.
